What it does: Basic attack on the aimed enemy.
Clarify more: Only ever a SUMMON acting on its own (Azir's Sand Soldier, Soraka's Child of the Star) - read Action Source. The champion's OWN attack is never this: it is a TRIGGER ('On Attack'), and what rides on it is 'Bonus on AA'. SHAPE IS PER-ROW: a projectile's own hitbox is in the row's AOE (hex) — a 1-hex bolt for most, Box 1.5x2 for Sona's wave. And it is the PROJECTILE's shape, never the shape of anything it bursts into: a burst is a separate step (trigger 'On Projectile Hit', source 'Step N Projectile').

What it does: Fires a projectile that homes on the aim target and applies its effect to it.
Clarify more: Has travel speed. Nothing on the way is touched - the projectile tracks the target until it lands. SHAPE IS PER-ROW: a projectile's own hitbox is in the row's AOE (hex) — 'default' is the ordinary 1-hex bolt, Box 1.5x2 for Sona's wave. And it is the PROJECTILE's shape, never the shape of anything it bursts into: a burst is a separate step (trigger 'On Projectile Hit', source 'Step N Projectile').

What it does: Fires ONE projectile that homes on the aim target; on hitting it, it re-homes on the next-nearest enemy, chaining Count times.
Clarify more: Count is the number of ENEMIES HIT (bounces) — one projectile that keeps re-targeting, NOT N simultaneous instances (which is what a Spread arranges). Katarina's 3-bounce blade; Zeri's chain lightning. Collision Target-Only: each hop touches only the unit it re-homed onto, nothing in between. Its OWN action rather than a Spread, because the bounce is a property of the DELIVERY, not an arrangement of copies — the user's call: 'putting Bounce chain as a spread means I can't have same-target + bounce in one row, which is wrong.' Shape per-row like every projectile; 1-hex is the default, so its AOE reads 'default'.

What it does: Fires a projectile that stops on the FIRST unit it hits.
Clarify more: Has travel speed. SHAPE IS PER-ROW: a projectile's own hitbox is in the row's AOE (hex) — a 1-hex bolt for most, Box 1.5x2 for Sona's wave. And it is the PROJECTILE's shape, never the shape of anything it bursts into: a burst is a separate step (trigger 'On Projectile Hit', source 'Step N Projectile').

What it does: Fires a projectile that passes THROUGH every unit in its path, hitting all of them.
Clarify more: Has travel speed. Replaced the old 'Wave', which was a duplicate of it. SHAPE IS PER-ROW: a projectile's own hitbox is in the row's AOE (hex) — a 1-hex bolt for most, Box 1.5x2 for Sona's wave. And it is the PROJECTILE's shape, never the shape of anything it bursts into: a burst is a separate step (trigger 'On Projectile Hit', source 'Step N Projectile').

What it does: The Count instances are arrayed in a ROW behind the caster and fire from their own slots, each piercing through the aim target and on into whatever is behind it.
Clarify more: Xayah's featherstrike. The user's name, and the user's call - 'how it look visually is so different from the other'. Mechanically it IS a Pierce Projectile with one axis changed: the anchor is fixed at 'rank -1' rather than 'centred', so the feathers are created behind her back rather than coming off her body. That is why it is its own action instead of a per-row Offset: Pierce Projectile's anchor is FIXED at centred, so one champion overriding it in Hero would have made this tab lie - Jhin, Sona and Quinn really are centred. Because each instance starts in a DIFFERENT slot and they all converge on ONE target (Spread = 'Same target'), they arrive at different angles and carry on through at different angles: a neat rank FANS OUT behind the target, which is the whole point and is testable. Shape is per-row, as with every projectile.

What it does: The hitbox appears directly ON the aim target's hex - nothing is fired and nothing travels.
Clarify more: Single-target on-target spawn only. The AOE-shaped 'spawn on target' cases moved to the shape templates (Circle/Box/Custom AOE), whose centre is derived from Aim Target the same way — nothing travels, so nothing between is touched.

What it does: Affects everyone inside an X-hex circle (X = the AOE (hex) column).
Clarify more: Centre is DERIVED from Action Source + Aim Target — the caster's hex when Aim=Self, the target's hex otherwise. The Offset column places the circle relative to that centre (usually 'centred'). Replaced the old 'Melee AOE' idea: a self-aimed Circle AOE already IS the melee case.

What it does: A cone-shaped hitbox projected in front of the caster, switched on when the skill is cast; everyone inside the cone is hit.
Clarify more: A real cone-shaped collider. Its origin is the caster's hex at the NARROW end and the shape grows AWAY from it, so the anchor is 'rear edge' - the user's call, with the picture: xxx / x / o. It read 'front edge' until then, which was backwards; that label's own definition used a cone as its example, which is how the error survived so long. Centre is derived from Action Source + Aim Target.

What it does: A rectangular AOE hitbox. Size in AOE (hex); the Offset column says where the box sits relative to its centre (see the 'Offset Types' tab).
Clarify more: One delivery with Circle/Cone — they differ only in Shape and the AOE size; centre is derived from Action Source + Aim Target.

What it does: An irregular AOE that does not fit Circle/Cone/Box; its shape is described in the AOE (hex) cell. Centre derived from Action Source + Aim Target; Offset as usual.
Clarify more: Use only for genuinely odd shapes. Offset said '—' until 2026-07-17 while its only Hero row said 'centred' - the tab claimed the action places no anchor and the row placed one. 'per row' is what it always meant: an irregular shape's anchor cannot be fixed here.

What it does: Beam that pierces ALL units in the path but applies its effect ONCE.
Clarify more: NO travel speed - it spawns on the unit and points at the target.

What it does: A laser that SWEEPS sideways through an arc while it is out, hitting everything the moving beam passes over.
Clarify more: Gwen's. The cone she appears to make is NOT a cone hitbox - it is this line sweeping, and it only LOOKS like a cone. This is the first action whose hitbox MOVES during the action: Delivery says where a hitbox spawns and Shape says what it is, but neither can say that it travels sideways. Carried in the Shape text for now; it deserves its own column if a second swept action ever appears. Replaced 'Cone Slash', which wrongly modelled the cone as a real collider.

What it does: A unit MOVES, carrying a hitbox: whatever it moves into is hit. The reposition and the hitbox are one action.
Clarify more: THE MOVE IS PART OF IT (2026-07-17, the user's call). It previously moved nothing and had to be paired with a separate 'Move' action - a rule ONE of its three users actually followed: Sion had the Move row, while Gangplank's Dreadway and K'Sante's flying body never did. A rule honoured 1-in-3 is not carrying its weight. KNOWN COST, accepted when the change was made: K'Sante's carrier does NOT move under its own power - his Knock Back throws it - and this action can no longer tell that apart from Sion charging in himself. If that distinction ever earns its keep it becomes a SECOND action, not a column. The CARRIER is whoever Action Source names and need not be the caster: Sion carries it himself; K'Sante's is the ENEMY he smashed (Source = 'Knocked-back enemy'), whose body hits everyone it flies through. Not an AOE - the units hit are exactly the ones the carrier passed through.

What it does: The CASTER becomes the bouncing hitbox: it charges to the aim target, then re-charges to the next-nearest enemy, chaining Count times — moving its own body each hop.
Clarify more: Zac. It is 'Bounce Homing Projectile + Charge' (the user's words): the same chaining as Bounce Homing Projectile, but the CARRIER moves (Charge) rather than a projectile being thrown — Katarina throws a blade and stays put, Zac's body bounces. Count is the number of bounces; Collision Target-Only, each hop touching only the enemy it re-homed onto. Its own action because 'the caster moves' is a property of the DELIVERY, not a per-row override. Shape per-row like every projectile (1-hex bolt = 'default').

What it does: Self grabs one enemy from EACH side and slams them together.
Clarify more: The effect applies only to the grabbed enemies. If only one side is occupied then only one is grabbed - and the effect is amplified by 50%.

What it does: Self smashes the aimed enemy across the board.
Clarify more: K'Sante's. HE projects no hitbox, so his Collision is None - the smash lands on the aimed enemy only. The FLYING BODY is a separate action ('Charge', Source = 'Knocked-back enemy'), which is what hits everyone on the way. Those were one row until the user pointed out the same problem on Sion; while they were lumped, this tab said None and the Hero row said Pierce-All, and both were right about a different half of it.

What it does: Waits for a previously thrown projectile to RETURN to the caster, then applies its effect to self.
Clarify more: Collision is Self, NOT None: something IS flying back and the unit it collides with is the caster. SHAPE IS PER-ROW: a projectile's own hitbox is in the row's AOE (hex) — a 1-hex bolt for most, Box 1.5x2 for Sona's wave. And it is the PROJECTILE's shape, never the shape of anything it bursts into: a burst is a separate step (trigger 'On Projectile Hit', source 'Step N Projectile').

What it does: Self plays an animation, then applies the stated effect or sets up the step that follows.
Clarify more: Projects no hitbox into the world, so its Collision is ALWAYS None - even when it is a melee strike on a target. Who it lands on is carried by Aim Target and Effect Recipient, not by the collision.

What it does: Self moves to the target hex - a reposition.
Clarify more: Projects nothing, so Collision is None. Normally just sets up the follow-up action that does the damage.

What it does: Self moves to the hex BEHIND the target - a reposition that ends up on the far side of it.
Clarify more: Projects nothing, so Collision is None. Normally just sets up the follow-up action that does the damage.

What it does: Self spawns a unit at the aim hex that STANDS there and attacks from its spot — it does not move.
Clarify more: Set 9 Zed's shadow, Azir's Sand Soldiers. The 2026-07-18 split of the old generic 'Summon' into three actions BY BEHAVIOUR (Static / Charge / Hero), the user's call: summons fall into stands-still / charges-in / acts-like-a-champion, and the behaviour is a property of the ACTION. Spawn is at-Target (on the aimed hex). The spawned unit is a SECOND action source for the steps that follow — its attacks are centred on IT, not on Self.

What it does: Self spawns unit(s) that CHARGE a target — they may spawn UNGRIDDED (float with no hex) and then charge in carrying a hitbox.
Clarify more: Naafiri's packmates, Gangplank's Dreadway. Replaced 'Summon (untethered)': being ungridded is itself part of the charge summon's nature (at-User/at-Target both claim a hex these units do not have, so Spawn is '—' and Aim Target may be '—'). The actual charge + hit is the follow-up step (e.g. 'Charge [collision=Target-Only]'), sourced from the summon. A second action source, like all three summons.

What it does: Self spawns a unit that acts like a CHAMPION — it WALKS the grid and auto-attacks on its own.
Clarify more: Set 10 Zed's untargetable Shadow; Soraka's Child of the Star. The difference from Static Summon is MOBILITY — a hero summon repositions and behaves like a real unit rather than attacking from a fixed spot. Spawn is at-Target (it appears on a hex, then moves). A second action source for the steps that follow.

What it does: Beam locked onto the aimed target ONLY, applying its effect over a duration.
Clarify more: NO travel speed. Nothing between the unit and the target is touched (Lux).

What it does: An effect that rides on an auto-attack that has ALREADY happened.
Clarify more: Pair it with Trigger = 'On Attack' / 'On 3rd Attack' / etc - the attack is the TRIGGER, so the action must not also be the attack. Projects NO hitbox: the auto-attack already delivered the hit, and this only adds an effect to it, so Collision is None. The effect is the point, and it need not be damage - Warwick HEALS ('Attacks heal for 30/35/40% AP'), Kalista impales, Zeri executes. Replaced 'Auto-Attack' as an action, which was circular ('On attack, do an auto-attack') - the user's call. If the attack itself is RESHAPED rather than merely added to, it is not this: Nilah's cone is a Cone AOE.

What it does: The unit attacks the target N times in QUICK SUCCESSION - N basic-attack-like hits in a row, not a real ability cast.
Clarify more: Bel'Veth: 6/6/25 rapid attacks on the lowest-HP enemy, carried in Count. It PERFORMS attacks, which is why it is its own action and not 'Bonus on AA' - and why it is no longer folded in with Auto-Attack (ranged), whose only users are summons. SHAPE IS PER-ROW: a projectile's own hitbox is in the row's AOE (hex) — a 1-hex bolt for most, Box 1.5x2 for Sona's wave. And it is the PROJECTILE's shape, never the shape of anything it bursts into: a burst is a separate step (trigger 'On Projectile Hit', source 'Step N Projectile').

What it does: A hitbox that STAYS for a duration, re-applying to whoever is inside it at each tick.
Clarify more: The difference from Circle AOE is WHERE THE DAMAGE COMES FROM, and it is testable: walk out of a Zone and it stops; walk away from a Circle AOE's poison and it keeps ticking, because that one rides on YOU. So the ACTION says which kind of duration a row means - a Zone's Effect Duration is the HITBOX's life, a Circle AOE's is the victim's status. Silco's chemicals sit on the ground for 6s; Garen's beyblade and Swain's aura are zones centred on the caster (Aim = Self). Its Effect Recipient is re-evaluated every tick, which is the whole point.

Legacy Action: AXES — fill each that applies, else —

Apply: 1-hex | circle | cone | box | custom | specify elsewhere
Spawn: (Hitbox only — 'specify elsewhere' = the row's AOE (hex) says it; every projectile)
(Hitbox only) That tab is the definition and VALIDATE checks Hero against it. Listing the values here as well would be a second copy of one fact, and two copies disagree given a chance.

Legacy Action: SHAPE: fixed for most actions and stated here — a Circle AOE is always a circle. But a PROJECTILE's shape is the ROW's ('specify elsewhere'): the same Pierce Projectile is a 1-hex bolt for Kayle and a 1.5-wide wave for Sona. SIZE is always per-row, in Hero's AOE (hex), which names a row of the 'AOE shape' tab. A BURST IS NOT A SHAPE: a projectile that explodes is TWO parts — the projectile, then a separate step (trigger 'On Projectile Hit', source 'Step N Projectile') for the AOE it becomes. Collision and Offset also stay in Hero where they vary ('per row' below).
